question: Liveness channel: how does a live observer learn a trial STARTED (the ledger only records completions)?
author: claude/design

question: HTTP server dependency for bench serve
author: claude/design

question: Blinding posture of the live view: arm identities are on the ledger in plaintext, but EVAL-7 human review depends on reviewer blindness
author: claude/design

question: Live transport: client polling of JSON endpoints vs SSE push
author: claude/design

rationale: Recommended option accepted via 2026-07-04 session directive ('spec this out properly and build the observability') following the same-day assessment: liveness is operational telemetry, not audit evidence — run.config.yaml precedent, zero hash-chained contract change; an additive trial_started event stays available as a v2 if audit-grade liveness is ever needed.
author: jyang

rationale: Read-only loopback tool: no new runtime dependency for GET+JSON; a framework becomes worth its weight only if auth or mutation endpoints arrive (a different story).
author: jyang

rationale: The run tier is legitimately unblinded (arm names are plaintext on trial events); aliasing arms in the UI would fake a blindness the data does not have. Follow the EVAL-9 openly-unblinded precedent: disclose on every render that watching disqualifies the viewer as an EVAL-7 blinded reviewer. Reviewer-safe views are deferred to the review-UI story.
author: jyang

rationale: Serial local trials change state at whole-trial cadence; atomic appends + small files make polling correct and simple. SSE is an additive transport swap later without changing the endpoint payloads.
author: jyang
